title: Woman arrested for fraud at roadworthy station
description: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The Hawks Serious...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The Hawks Serious Commercial Crime Investigation team based in George arrested a 43-year-old woman on charges of fraud on Friday 26 April.


> Lungiswa Tania Klaas appeared at the Plettenberg Bay Magistrate's Court on the same day and was granted bail of R5 000.


The matter is postponed to 6 June for a Regional Court date.

According to a spokesperson for the Hawks Lt. Col Siyabulela Vukubi it is reported that during the period 2021 and 2022 an intelligence driven operation was conducted at a roadworthy vehicle testing station in Plettenberg Bay.

"Investigation revealed that Klaas received money to facilitate and provide a roadworthy certificate for a vehicle that was never present and inspected by the roadworthy station," said Vukubi.

Klaas allegedly made use of a contact at a roadworthy station in Port Elizabeth to do the fraudulent roadworthy transaction.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
